useful_deltas.summary: Added a fail-closed immutable Nasdaq cash-dividend archive and as-of provider that preserves declarationDate as known_at for supported eventful listings without converting empty or unsupported history into coverage.
Moved archive coverage to the earliest retained announcement time so the first announced pre-ex event is servable, while rejecting loaded announcements later than snapshot observation time.

critic_findings.finding: coverage_start based on the earliest ex-date orphaned the first event between its known_at and ex-date.
critic_findings.rationale: Construction and loader validation now use min(known_at); a boundary test proves the first event is returned after announcement and before ex-date, and loader timing rejects known_at later than observed_at.

critic_findings.finding: Split-unadjusted Nasdaq amounts can distort deep-history assignment thresholds against split-adjusted underlyings.
critic_findings.rationale: Rejected as an unaddressed calibrated-result defect because this wake makes no historical assignment-count or edge claim. The accepted limitation is explicit in docs/DIVIDEND_EVENT_DATA_BOUNDARY.md and remains a qualification gate before any such claim.

critic_findings.finding: MU and AAL inventory counts were not backed by normalized on-disk archives.
critic_findings.rationale: The boundary document labels these as live source inventory, not provider eligibility. Only AAPL is archived/cited as a supported example, and NEXT permits MU only after normalized capture.

critic_findings.finding: Nasdaq aggregation may include preferred or wrong security-class rows and lacks a positive identity proof.
critic_findings.rationale: Rejected as permission to broaden the source: the provider remains exact-symbol archived L0/single-source plumbing, while independent completeness and security-class identity are explicit prerequisites in NEXT and project truth.

critic_findings.finding: Readiness LATEST still represented the prior 1806 inventory seed and needed a full 1835 finalizer outcome.
critic_findings.rationale: Rejected as a remaining defect after finalization: reports/readiness/LATEST.md is superseded with the repaired 1835 outcome, BUILD/L0 judgment, final verification, and one current NEXT.

critic_findings.finding: A Nasdaq-specific contiguous-declaration and empty-history pitfall should be promoted for reuse.
critic_findings.rationale: Rejected as missing durable residue after finalization: the trader-self-evolution profile skill now records contiguous cash/USD retention, first-N/A truncation, empty/unsupported None semantics, earliest-known_at coverage, and amount/security-class claim limits.

data_dependencies: An independent issuer or filing announcement source is required to qualify AAPL event completeness and security-class identity; MU requires a normalized archive before comparison.
Split-adjusted amount/spot qualification is required before deep-history assignment counts can be treated as calibrated evidence.
Observed historical option surfaces remain required for evidence above L0.
Forward TSLL option archive remains one of three required distinct New York market dates for replay plumbing.
next: Cross-check archived Nasdaq cash-dividend events for AAPL (and MU only if a normalized archive is captured first) against one independent issuer/filing announcement source; qualify per-symbol provenance only if completeness and security-class identity are proved, otherwise keep the provider L0/single-source and redirect to another open historical-underlying or simulator-capability route.
